title: E SAKUGA Evangelion New Theatrical Edition Q Volume 1
display_title: E SAKUGA Evangelion New Theatrical Edition Q Volume 1
wikitext:
[]: E-SAKUGA Evangelion New Theatrical Edition: Q Volume 1             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue May 05, 2015 7:03 pm tweet-body — twitter-user (@username) date document.querySelectorAll('blockquote.twitter-tweet > a').forEach((t) => t.href = t.href.replace('x.com', 'twitter.com')) If it's on iTunes Japan, I guess that's how they'd release it internationally!

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Tue May 05, 2015 7:13 pm Darn and I thought they were going to make a Q/3.0 Manga.

[]:             Quote Post by Sicarius VI » Tue May 05, 2015 9:08 pm Wait I'm confused and forgive me for being ignorant. So they are releasing a book based around/retelling 3.0? Or am I completely mistaken?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue May 05, 2015 9:28 pm Sicarius VI wrote: So they are releasing a book based around/retelling 3.0? Or am I completely mistaken?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue May 05, 2015 9:36 pm Anybody preordering this from the U.S. iTunes link on Minna no Eva Fan? Curious on people's thoughts about this!

[]:             Quote Post by Sicarius VI » Tue May 05, 2015 9:44 pm Thanks Reichu. If that's the case then it would be pretty cool if there are any deleted scenes of 3.0 in storyboard format or just in general shots they didn't use. Just in general it seems like a cool Behind the Scenes book.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Tue May 05, 2015 10:10 pm Groundwork books have neither deleted scenes nor storyboards. It's only animation with the occasional layout.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Wed May 06, 2015 1:11 am Reichu wrote: This seems to be kind of like the Groundwork books, showcasing the drawings used for the animation process, but digital. Not sure why there are screencaps, though.

[]: Trailer             Quote Post by Konoro » Thu May 07, 2015 7:06 pm E-SAKUGA allows users to see key frames serially according to the trailer. https://m.youtube.com/watch?v=Lo3WbUAk1sg Pretty cool!

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed May 13, 2015 10:29 am Pity it's iPad/Mac only. I'd buy it in a heartbeat if I could use it on my phone.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Wed May 13, 2015 10:39 am Same here. I may negotiate for my wife's old iPad (I bought her a new iPad Air last birthday).

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed May 13, 2015 10:43 am I have an iMac, and an iPhone. Why don’t I have this yet? CURSE YOU BILLS!!!!

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Wed May 13, 2015 11:10 am You should be able to get it on the iMac.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed May 13, 2015 11:26 am $20 isn’t bad for this. I still want the book edition of it, though. But I’ll also buy this.

[]: Cool that people are interested!!             Quote Post by Konoro » Wed May 20, 2015 2:20 am I'm actually the translator for E-SAKUGA. I will be presenting in Fanimecon coming Sunday May 24 from 2pm to 3pm at Room 4 if anyone of you are interested to learn more about us and E-SAKUGA. You can ask us questions through twitter and FB too! Or, we try to follow this forum post too. FB: https://www.facebook.com/esakuga?notif_t=fbpage_admin Twitter: https://twitter.com/ESAKUGA Feedback is welcome!! The book version is great as a collectible, but the interactiveness of E-SAKUGA gives you a more intuitive understanding of how the key frames are animated. In that sense, the two are quite different. (plus, for personal use, you can print) Our goal is to help anime fans enjoy the techniques of creating anime alongside the story and design. If you get E-SAKUGA, I'd like you to check out the first "About SAKUGA" page. It's the first time SAKUGA was defined by people in the anime industry and then translated into English:) Researchers and animators were involved, and a translator who understands anime (me) translated it. If these books sell well, we will be able to create more. We just started making these and your support is... well... essential. Our primary target is YOU, or anime fans abroad:) If you can help us get known to people outside of Japan, you will be our hero. We believe in the product. We just don't know how to reach out to you yet. I hope iPhone support iBooks soon... Thank you! And hope you keep enjoying anime and perhaps E-SAKUGA! And Evangelion, of course:) EvaQ is actually our second and newest E-SAKUGA. Giovanni's Island was our first one. Giovanni's Island may be more interesting in terms of key frames and techniques employed. EvaQ https://itunes.apple.com/jp/book/anime-e-sakuga-evangelion/id991487083?mt=11&ign-mpt=uo%3D4 Giovanni's Island https://itunes.apple.com/jp/book/anime-giovannis-island-e-sakuga/id952136583?mt=11&ign-mpt=uo%3D4 ありがとう。

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Wed May 20, 2015 6:26 am Thank you very much for both your efforts and reaching out to the community. This would be a lovely product if not for the fact that it's stuck on Apple devices.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu May 21, 2015 6:30 pm Thank you very much for contacting us--we contacted you on the Evageeks Twitter, too. I myself don't have a smart phone, but agree that multiple platforms (iPhone, Android) would be a wonderful idea for the future--whenever possible. ありがとうございます！

[]: Thank you!!             Quote Post by Konoro » Fri May 22, 2015 10:04 am Thank you for your encouraging comments!:) I could see how the platform is a big issue... As far as I know the primary concern is the cost for building a new app opposed to using the iBooks features. But with enough demand, it may happen. Currently, this is like a probono project trying to save anime related art in digital format and helping fans to enjoy the techniques in anime. If we get more traction, we could do more. That's our hope! よろしくお願いします。

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Fri May 22, 2015 1:44 pm Even though I already own the physical Q Groundwork books, I would gladly buy them again in the interactive digital format if it was available on platforms I already own. I wish you the best of luck with these projects. It's very nice to see people care about the often tedious creative process and not just the final product.

[]:             Quote Post by cyharding » Thu Sep 17, 2015 10:18 am I'm bumping this because apparently they released volume back on Aug 28. [url]http://www.esakuga.net/press-release/e-sakuga-evangelion-3-0-you-can-not-redo-2-aug-28th-fri-global-release/[/url]
